steps.title: The Holes
steps.text: A weak square is one your opponent can't defend with a pawn. Usually the result of a careless pawn push. If a knight lands there and can't be kicked, it becomes an immovable strong point.

steps.title: Color Complexes
steps.text: Deeper than individual squares are "color complexes" — groups of squares of ONE color that are systematically weak. If your opponent's pawns are all on light squares, his dark squares are weak. Your dark-squared pieces become monsters.
